meta.title: Portfolio GTM Operating System Mega Report
meta.description: Lifecycle capture, evidence map, paused gates, and automation roadmap for Kingly Studio, Argo, Drone, and Lev OSS.

props.title: Portfolio GTM Operating System
props.subtitle: Evidence-first discovery across Kingly Studio, Argo, Drone, Lev OSS, brand assets, source systems, and automation lanes.

props.content: This pass converted the original GTM brain dump into lifecycle state: 10 parent issues, 10 execution subtasks, a workstream capture ledger, six specialist read-only investigations, and explicit [tbd] placeholders where evidence, access, founder judgment, NDA review, or counsel input is still required.

props.title: Current
props.content: Local evidence exists, but it is fragmented: repo-local marketing folders, mounted Google Drive brand and marketing folders, Argo investor/product docs, Drone proof and demo docs, Lev pre-release OSS docs, Notion search results, and Paperclip agency-binding projections. Several useful assets are still only filename-level inventory because binary review and approval are pending.

props.title: Ideal
props.content: One source-owned GTM operating system: Drive owns binary/source assets, local repos own execution proof, bd/workstream YAML own lifecycle state, Paperclip owns org graph and run visibility, Notion owns curated operating knowledge after workspace separation, and Lev automations move signals into reviewed briefs instead of publishing blindly.

props.label: Workstream
props.title: The 10-item todo map after capture
props.subtitle: Captured means enough evidence exists for planning. Paused means the next move requires access, founder decision, legal/counsel review, or NDA review.

Source freshness is source-by-source [tbd] until citation review.
Founder decisions required; no assumptions allowed.
Depends on source-of-truth, approval gates, and content safety classes.
Target workspace/database IDs and safe mutation rules are [tbd].
Lev asset ownership/controller facts are [tbd]; counsel required.

props.title: No blind mutation
props.content: Notion, Paperclip, Google Drive, Slack, Discord, and Gmail stay read/analysis surfaces until target IDs, account boundaries, and approval rules are explicit. Missing facts stay [tbd].

props.title: 1. GTM context spine
props.content: Product truth, ICPs, proof points, brand rules, forbidden claims, source registry. Every generation cites source context and flags [tbd].

props.title: 2. Market signal radar
props.content: Monitor competitors, OSS activity, funding/news, pricing/docs changes, AI-search descriptions, and category shifts.

props.title: 3. Content workbench
props.content: Draft, remix, adapt, and package content from approved proof cards. Human review before publish.

props.title: 4. Signal-to-action queue
props.content: Turn monitored signals into proposed actions: account brief, outreach draft, landing-page variant, partner target, issue, or opportunity.

props.title: 5. AI visibility loop
props.content: Track how LLMs describe Kingly, Argo, Drone, Lev, and competitors. Report share-of-voice, misdescription, and missing source assets.

props.label: Market Research
props.title: AI-native GTM patterns that matter here
Brand context as infrastructure
props.content: Jasper, Writer, and similar platforms are moving brand voice, guardrails, and institutional knowledge into reusable AI context. For Kingly, this maps to a source-owned GTM context spine.

props.title: GTM workflows as functions
props.content: Clay and Copy.ai-style systems turn repeatable enrichment, account research, and GTM operations into callable workflows. Kingly can package internal proof into reusable client and product motions.

props.title: Signal-based intelligence
props.content: Common Room, HubSpot, and Clay point toward GTM triggered by buyer, product, community, CRM, docs, and OSS signals instead of manual campaign guessing.

props.title: AI-assisted, not AI-owned
props.content: AI is strong at drafts, variants, summaries, metadata, and reports. Humans still own taste, positioning, category POV, legal claims, and final publishing.

props.title: Competitive deltas with citations
props.content: Crayon and Semrush-style monitoring is useful only when changes include source URLs, dates, severity, and a human accept/reject/watch decision.

props.title: AI visibility monitoring
props.content: AEO/GEO matters because LLM answers may describe the portfolio before prospects reach the website. Track descriptions and citation quality, not one-off rank.

props.label: Paused

props.label: Founder Interview
props.title: Questions that should not be guessed
Lead narrative
props.content: Which should lead external GTM for the next 90 days: Kingly Studio, Argo, Lev OSS, or a portfolio-level AI-native operating-system story? [tbd]

props.title: Proof boundaries
props.content: Which Drone materials are cleared for public, anonymized, private-sales-only, or blocked use? [tbd]

props.title: Canonical systems
props.content: Which Drive folder, Notion workspace, and Paperclip project should own GTM operations? [tbd]

props.title: M3 access
props.content: What is the M3 Studio access method and local path convention? [tbd]

props.title: Lev governance
props.content: Who currently owns or controls Lev code, trademarks, domains, package names, GitHub orgs, and funds? [tbd]

props.title: Operator rhythm
props.content: What cadence should the COO/VA run: daily asset intake, weekly content review, monthly AI visibility, or another rhythm? [tbd]

Resume workstream org-gtm-operating-system on the M3 Studio. Read the local Kingly/Argo/Drone/Lev folders and return a read-only inventory. Do not copy files. Do not assume mirrored paths. For each asset, report: domain, absolute path, account/source, what it appears to contain, modified time if useful, freshness, owner if known, conflict with this-machine sources, and [tbd] for unknowns. Classify outputs for Kingly brand, Argo, Drone, Lev OSS, Notion/Paperclip/GDrive ops, and personal ideas. Mark anything requiring NDA/legal/founder review as paused.
